name: The Pig and the Lady
tagline: Vietnamese-Hawaiian fusion that put Chinatown dining on the map — Chef Andrew Le's pho French dip is genius.
mustOrder: Pho French dip — prime rib dipped in pho broth, absolute game changer
Crispy crepe banh xeo — stuffed with shrimp, pork, and herbs
Smoked meat pho — rich bone broth with brisket and all the fixings
parking: Street parking on Smith or Hotel St. Chinatown garage at 1130 N Nimitz Hwy is cheapest. Meter parking free after 6pm.
talkStory: Chef Andrew Le, son of Vietnamese refugees, started selling pho at the KCC Farmers Market and blew up from there. His Chinatown restaurant earned a James Beard semifinalist nomination for Best Chef West. The pho French dip — prime rib dipped in pho broth — is genius-level fusion. Saturday farmers market booth still has lines. Dinner reservations recommended; the bar takes walk-ins. Friday and Saturday nights have serious energy in the Chinatown dining room.
